title: digitalis × pelia
intro: Digitalis × valinii, formerly known as Digitalis × pelia, is a hybrid plant of the foxglove species, a member of the plantain family. The leaves of this plant contain digoxin and digitoxin, compounds with medicinal properties that have been used to treat various heart conditions, such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The medicinal properties of Digitalis × valinii have been shown to improve heart function and reduce symptoms of heart failure, making it a valuable herbal remedy for cardiovascular health. Digitalis × valinii can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried extract, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
